message:
chore(beads): update issue tracking metadata

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -311,7 +311,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-1i2ge.8.5","title":"[mission][H5] Commit-phase executor with deterministic ordering and barrier semantics","description":"## Objective
Implement commit-phase orchestration with deterministic ordering, barrier semantics, and robust outcome capture.

## Scope
- Execute prepared step batches in deterministic order.
- Enforce barrier semantics between dependent batches.
- Record commit receipts, partial-failure boundaries, and terminal commit outcome.
- Integrate pause/resume/abort and duplicate-dispatch protections.

## Deliverables
- Commit runtime engine and barrier scheduler.
- Commit receipt protocol with per-step and per-batch status.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Commit ordering and barrier behavior are deterministic and documented.
2. Partial failures are detected at precise boundaries with enough context for compensation planning.
3. Duplicate dispatch prevention remains intact under retries and restarts.
4. Unit tests cover nominal commit, barrier wait, timeout, and mid-batch failure paths.
5. Integration tests validate pause/resume/abort interactions during commit.
6. Logs include commit_batch_start/end, step_receipt, and commit_boundary markers.
7. Deliverables must include comprehensive unit tests plus executable e2e script `tests/e2e/test_mission_tx_commit.sh` and detailed structured logs under `tests/e2e/logs/mission_tx_commit_*.jsonl` proving nominal, edge, and failure-path behavior.
8. Structured log contract for this bead requires fields: timestamp, component, scenario_id, correlation_id, decision_path, input_summary, outcome, reason_code, error_code, artifact_path.","notes":"Future-self note: commit runtime is the blast-radius control point; preserve strict ordering and explicit boundaries.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RubyLynx","created_at":"2026-02-22T23:29:31.803887Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-25T07:39:57.830227Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T07:39:57.830211Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dispatch","e2e-tests","idempotency","integration-tests","logging","mission","quality-gate","recovery","runtime","test-coverage","test-logging","transactional-execution","tx","unit-tests","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.5","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.5","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.5","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.5","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.3.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.5","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.3.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.5","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.5","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1500,"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.5","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"RubyLynx validation (2026-02-25): All acceptance criteria met.

Implementation: execute_commit_phase() in plan.rs with deterministic step ordering, barrier semantics (skip after first failure), kill-switch blocking, pause suspension. Types: TxCommitStepInput, TxCommitStepResult, TxCommitReport, TxCommitOutcome (FullyCommitted/ImmediateFailure/PartialFailure/KillSwitchBlocked/PauseSuspended).

Validation: 33 unit tests (commit_* in plan.rs) + 13 property tests (proptest_commit_phase.rs) — all pass locally (rch fail-open).
Commands:
- CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-rubylynx cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib commit_
- CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-rubylynx cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test proptest_commit_phase

E2E harness: tests/e2e/test_ft_1i2ge_8_5.sh with structured logging. Wired into scripts/e2e_test.sh with run_scenario_ft_1i2ge_8_5() + dispatch case.","created_at":"2026-02-25T07:39:53Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-1i2ge.8.6","title":"[mission][H6] Compensation planner and automatic rollback engine","description":"## Objective
Implement compensation and rollback runtime for automatic recovery from partial commit failures.

## Scope
- Build compensation plan from executed subset + predefined compensators.
- Execute compensation with deterministic ordering and safety gates.
- Support auto-rollback and operator-forced rollback modes.
- Emit final rollback outcome with unresolved residual risk markers if compensation is incomplete.

## Deliverables
- Compensation planner and executor.
- Rollback policy (auto-trigger thresholds, forced mode, failure escalation).

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Partial commit failure triggers compensation plan generation without manual intervention.
2. Compensation steps are policy-gated and auditable like forward steps.
3. Rollback terminal outcomes are explicit: rolled_back, rollback_partial, rollback_failed.
4. Unit tests cover successful rollback, compensation-denied, and compensation-timeout paths.
5. Integration tests validate end-to-end recovery from injected mid-commit failures.
6. Logs include compensation_plan_hash, compensation_step_receipts, and rollback_outcome.
7. Deliverables must include comprehensive unit tests plus executable e2e script `tests/e2e/test_mission_tx_rollback.sh` and detailed structured logs under `tests/e2e/logs/mission_tx_rollback_*.jsonl` proving nominal, edge, and failure-path behavior.
8. Structured log contract for this bead requires fields: timestamp, component, scenario_id, correlation_id, decision_path, input_summary, outcome, reason_code, error_code, artifact_path.","notes":"Future-self note: rollback correctness is existential for operator trust; optimize for correctness and observability before speed.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RubyLynx","created_at":"2026-02-22T23:29:32.302854Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-25T08:13:43.420755Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T08:13:43.420741Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["compensation","e2e-tests","integration-tests","logging","mission","quality-gate","recovery","rollback","safety","test-coverage","test-logging","transactional-execution","tx","unit-tests","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.6","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.6","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.4.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.6","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.6","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1511,"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.6","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"RubyLynx validation (2026-02-25): All acceptance criteria met.

Implementation: execute_compensation_phase() in plan.rs with reverse-ordinal ordering, barrier semantics, kill-switch awareness. Types: TxCompensationStepInput, TxCompensationStepResult, TxCompensationReport, TxCompensationOutcome (FullyRolledBack/ImmediateFailure/PartialFailure/NothingToCompensate/NoDefinedCompensations). Also fixed lib compilation by adding stub cx/outcome modules for builds without asupersync-runtime/web/sync features.

Validation: 19 unit tests + 13 property tests (proptest_compensation.rs) — all pass locally.
Commands:
- CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-rubylynx-fresh cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib compensation
- CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-rubylynx-fresh cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test proptest_compensation

E2E: tests/e2e/test_ft_1i2ge_8_6.sh. Wired into scripts/e2e_test.sh.","created_at":"2026-02-25T08:12:01Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-1i2ge.8.7","title":"[mission][H7] Durable idempotency, dedupe, and resume invariants","description":"## Objective
Guarantee restart-safe idempotency and resume semantics across prepare/commit/compensation paths.

## Scope
- Transaction-level and step-level idempotency keys.
- Deduplication across process restart and replayed commands.
- Resume protocol that rehydrates tx state from ledger safely.
- Guardrails to prevent double-commit and double-compensation.

## Deliverables
- Idempotency key policy and persistence strategy.
- Resume/recovery state reconstruction module.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Retries/restarts do not create duplicate forward or compensating side effects.
2. Resume logic reconstructs tx state accurately from ledger and revalidates stale preconditions before continuing.
3. Idempotency behavior is deterministic under concurrent duplicate submissions.
4. Unit tests cover key derivation, dedupe logic, and resume decision branches.
5. Property/concurrency tests validate no-double-commit/no-double-compensation under randomized interleavings.
6. Logs include idempotency_key, dedupe_decision, and resume_path reason codes.
7. Deliverables must include comprehensive unit tests plus executable e2e script `tests/e2e/test_mission_tx_idempotency_resume.sh` and detailed structured logs under `tests/e2e/logs/mission_tx_idempotency_resume_*.jsonl` proving nominal, edge, and failure-path behavior.
8. Structured log contract for this bead requires fields: timestamp, component, scenario_id, correlation_id, decision_path, input_summary, outcome, reason_code, error_code, artifact_path.","notes":"Future-self note: this bead is where transactional claims become real under operational restarts and retries.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RubyLynx","created_at":"2026-02-22T23:29:32.800807Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-25T08:36:13.010114Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T08:36:13.010102Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["concurrency","e2e-tests","idempotency","integration-tests","logging","mission","property-tests","quality-gate","recovery","test-coverage","test-logging","transactional-execution","tx","unit-tests","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.7","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.3.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.7","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.7","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1519,"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.7","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"RubyLynx validation: 66 unit tests (tx_idempotency module) ✓, 15 proptest (idempotency_resume) ✓, 25 proptest (tx_idempotency) ✓ = 106 tests total. Covers: IdempotencyKey determinism/uniqueness/format/compensation, StepOutcome predicates/serde, TxPhase transitions/terminal/serde, TxExecutionLedger ordinals/hash-chain/duplicate-rejection/sealed/serde, DeduplicationGuard capacity/eviction, ResumeContext disjoint-sets/plan-coverage, IdempotencyStore lifecycle, cross-instance dedup, full tx lifecycle (plan→prepare→commit→complete). E2e harness test_ft_1i2ge_8_7.sh (29 scenarios) wired into scripts/e2e_test.sh.","created_at":"2026-02-25T08:36:09Z"}]}
-{"id":"ft-1i2ge.8.8","title":"[mission][H8] Control surfaces for ft robot tx and MCP parity","description":"## Objective
Expose transactional execution as stable user/machine control surfaces.

## Scope
- CLI/Robot endpoints: `tx plan`, `tx run`, `tx rollback`, `tx show`.
- MCP parity for agent integrations.
- Stable JSON/TOON envelopes and error semantics.
- Human-readable explain output + machine-parseable receipt views.

## Deliverables
- CLI command family wiring.
- Robot/MCP handlers and schema docs.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. All four tx commands are implemented and schema-stable across supported interfaces.
2. Error envelopes and reason codes are consistent across CLI, Robot, and MCP.
3. `tx show` surfaces complete intent/receipt/rollback timeline without hidden fields.
4. Unit tests validate parser/argument/schema handling.
5. Integration tests validate end-to-end command flow to runtime and ledger.
6. Contract tests include golden fixtures for payload stability and rich diagnostic logging.
7. Deliverables must include comprehensive unit tests plus executable e2e script `tests/e2e/test_mission_tx_interfaces.sh` and detailed structured logs under `tests/e2e/logs/mission_tx_interfaces_*.jsonl` proving nominal, edge, and failure-path behavior.
8. Structured log contract for this bead requires fields: timestamp, component, scenario_id, correlation_id, decision_path, input_summary, outcome, reason_code, error_code, artifact_path.","notes":"Future-self note: interface clarity is part of safety; ambiguous control surfaces create accidental misuse.","status":"blocked","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CoralIsland","created_at":"2026-02-22T23:29:33.311982Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-09T06:01:11.036480Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["api","cli","e2e-tests","golden-tests","integration-tests","logging","mcp","mission","quality-gate","robot-mode","test-coverage","test-logging","transactional-execution","tx","unit-tests","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.8","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.8","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.8","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.5.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.8","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.5.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.8","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.8","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1616,"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.8","author":"CalmSpring","text":"Validation status for ft-1i2ge.8.8: blocked by rch probe/status mismatch causing unsafe fallback risk.\n\nWork completed in this pass:\n1) Hardened tests/e2e/test_mission_tx_interfaces.sh with guarded preflight (rch check + probe + status artifact capture) and strict refusal to continue when probe reports zero reachable workers (RCH-E101 mismatch).\n2) Added explicit local-fallback guard for each robot command invocation: fail on any '[RCH] local' marker in stdout/stderr.\n3) Added remote smoke-check artifact wiring and structured logging for preflight transitions.\n\nValidation evidence:\n- Fast-fail guarded run (no local cargo): tests/e2e/logs/mission_tx_interfaces_20260227_203615.jsonl (+ .check.log .probe.log .status.json)\n- Prior exploratory run before strict mismatch refusal: tests/e2e/logs/mission_tx_interfaces_20260227_203322.jsonl\n\nCurrent blocker:\n- rch workers probe reports all workers connection_failed while rch check/status reports ready capacity. To honor offload-only policy, script now stops before tx command execution in this mismatch state.","created_at":"2026-02-28T01:36:40Z"},{"id":1618,"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.8","author":"CalmSpring","text":"Follow-up blocker evidence (2026-02-28): after additional hardening, rch still fails open during first tx command compile/run.\n\nKey hardening now in tests/e2e/test_mission_tx_interfaces.sh:\n- Force rch temp path via TMPDIR=/tmp for all rch calls (fixes probe false negatives from long ControlPath).\n- Use repo-local test workspace path under tests/e2e/tmp/... so worker can access it after sync.\n- Add live offload guard in run_robot_json that monitors stderr/stdout and terminates command immediately on '[RCH] local'.\n\nLatest run evidence:\n- tests/e2e/logs/mission_tx_interfaces_20260227_204958.jsonl\n- tests/e2e/logs/mission_tx_interfaces_20260227_204958.show_nominal.stderr.log\n\nOutcome:\n- Preflight passed with healthy workers and remote smoke.\n- First command (show_nominal) ran remotely for several minutes, then emitted '[RCH] local (remote execution failed)'.\n- Live guard terminated the command and prevented prolonged local compile.\n\nStatus remains blocked until rch fail-open behavior is resolved for this command path.","created_at":"2026-02-28T01:55:47Z"},{"id":1635,"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.8","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"PearlCave progress (2026-02-28): hardened tests/e2e/test_mission_tx_interfaces.sh to enforce offload-only semantics and richer preflight diagnostics.

Changes made:
1) Added explicit preflight artifact capture: rch check, rch status --workers --jobs --json, rch workers probe --all --json, and remote smoke command logs.
2) Added mismatch gate: if rch status reports healthy workers but probe reports zero reachable workers, fail with reason_code=rch_health_probe_mismatch and error_code=RCH-E101.
3) Added local fallback guardrails:
   - Preflight smoke command fails immediately on '[RCH] local'.
   - run_robot_json now monitors stdout/stderr while command is running and terminates the command immediately if '[RCH] local' appears.
4) Added explicit non-zero command failure logging with artifact pointers and reason_code=command_failed.
5) Switched workspace path to repo-local tests/e2e/tmp/<run_id> and forced TMPDIR=/tmp for shorter temp paths in this harness.

Validation artifacts:
- Syntax/lint: bash -n + shellcheck on tests/e2e/test_mission_tx_interfaces.sh passed.
- Run with fallback guard behavior observed previously: tests/e2e/logs/mission_tx_interfaces_20260228_013716.*
- Latest run after hardening: tests/e2e/logs/mission_tx_interfaces_20260228_014157.jsonl
  - show_nominal failed with explicit remote error (no fallback): worker stderr shows repeated 'No space left on device' during zstd-sys compile on remote host.
  - Artifact: tests/e2e/logs/mission_tx_interfaces_20260228_014157.show_nominal.stderr.log

Current blocker:
- Remote worker disk exhaustion ('No space left on device') prevents successful remote cargo run for this suite despite healthy preflight.
- Harness now reports this deterministically with command-level failure artifacts instead of drifting into local compile fallback.","created_at":"2026-02-28T06:43:27Z"},{"id":1885,"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.8","author":"CoralIsland","text":"Progress update (2026-03-04): hardened tests/e2e/test_mission_tx_interfaces.sh and revalidated under rch-only execution.\n\nChanges in this pass:\n1) Remote temp dir safety + deterministic fallback taxonomy:\n   - REMOTE_TMPDIR default changed to /tmp/rch-mission-tx-interfaces (absolute).\n   - Added explicit RCH-E100 classification for SSH connectivity fallback (reason_code=rch_worker_connect_failed) in smoke + run_robot_json path.\n   - Included RCH-E100 in one-shot retry preflight set.\n2) Fixed remote workspace path bug:\n   - Added WORKSPACE_REL_DIR and switched robot invocations to --workspace tests/e2e/tmp/... (repo-relative) instead of absolute local /Users/... path.\n   - Added explicit workspace_path_unwritable mapping to FT-7010 for non-zero command exits.\n\nValidation:\n- bash -n tests/e2e/test_mission_tx_interfaces.sh ✅\n- shellcheck tests/e2e/test_mission_tx_interfaces.sh ✅\n- E2E run: bash tests/e2e/test_mission_tx_interfaces.sh (run id 20260304_181034)\n  - Preflight passed (rch check + disk capacity)\n  - show_nominal attempt1 timed out at rch external timeout 300s\n  - retry preflight recovered\n  - show_nominal retry2 timed out again at 300s and correctly failed with\n    reason_code=rch_remote_timeout, error_code=RCH-E107\n  - Artifacts: tests/e2e/logs/mission_tx_interfaces_20260304_181034.jsonl and .show_nominal.retry2.stderr.log\n\nCurrent blocker:\n- rch external timeout (300s) is insufficient for first-time cargo run -p frankenterm --bin ft in this environment; harness correctly refuses fail-open local fallback.","created_at":"2026-03-04T23:22:15Z"},{"id":2038,"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.8","author":"WhiteSnow","text":"WhiteSnow taking a bounded support slice on tests/e2e/test_mission_tx_interfaces.sh. Goal: reduce or eliminate rch-timeout/local-fallback risk by tightening the remote build/run strategy in the tx interface harness without broadening scope beyond H8 validation infrastructure. I will reserve the harness file, inspect the current remote invocation pattern, and report back with concrete evidence/artifacts.","created_at":"2026-03-09T05:32:47Z"},{"id":2041,"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.8","author":"WhiteSnow","text":"Progress update: confirmed the current blocker is not only the tx harness. RCH for this repo is using a user-level build_timeout_sec=300, while prior H8 evidence already showed first-time remote ft builds exceeding 300s and failing open. I have now added a repo-scoped override in .rch/config.toml to raise build_timeout_sec to 900 so frankenterm offload policy can tolerate cold remote CLI builds without immediately degrading into local fallback. I also surveyed the worker fleet topology: 7/8 workers currently have the required sibling /home/ubuntu/rch/frankenterm/{frankenredis,frankentui} roots, but vmi1227854 is missing both, so remote selection remains partially nondeterministic until that worker is repaired or excluded. Next step: rerun tests/e2e/test_mission_tx_interfaces.sh under the new timeout and capture whether the remaining blocker is only the misconfigured worker or something deeper in remote cold-build time.","created_at":"2026-03-09T05:52:15Z"},{"id":2043,"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.8","author":"WhiteSnow","text":"Follow-up correction: the repo-scoped timeout override path did not hold up under verification. I tested three variants: (1) the shared daemon, (2) an isolated daemon on a separate socket, and (3) an isolated daemon with a temp HOME carrying a copied user config where build_timeout_sec=900. In all cases, `rch exec -- cargo build --help` still logged `timeout_secs: 300` for cargo-build classification. I therefore reverted both the temporary `.rch/config.toml` compilation override and my speculative build-once edit to `tests/e2e/test_mission_tx_interfaces.sh`, leaving the repo code unchanged. Net new evidence from this support slice: the 300s cargo-build timeout appears to be sourced from RCH behavior outside repo-scoped config, and worker `vmi1227854` remains missing `/home/ubuntu/rch/frankenterm/frankenredis` and `/home/ubuntu/rch/frankenterm/frankentui` while the other 7 workers had both roots during survey.","created_at":"2026-03-09T06:01:11Z"}]}
+{"id":"ft-1i2ge.8.8","title":"[mission][H8] Control surfaces for ft robot tx and MCP parity","description":"## Objective
Expose transactional execution as stable user/machine control surfaces.

## Scope
- CLI/Robot endpoints: `tx plan`, `tx run`, `tx rollback`, `tx show`.
- MCP parity for agent integrations.
- Stable JSON/TOON envelopes and error semantics.
- Human-readable explain output + machine-parseable receipt views.

## Deliverables
- CLI command family wiring.
- Robot/MCP handlers and schema docs.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. All four tx commands are implemented and schema-stable across supported interfaces.
2. Error envelopes and reason codes are consistent across CLI, Robot, and MCP.
3. `tx show` surfaces complete intent/receipt/rollback timeline without hidden fields.
4. Unit tests validate parser/argument/schema handling.
5. Integration tests validate end-to-end command flow to runtime and ledger.
6. Contract tests include golden fixtures for payload stability and rich diagnostic logging.
7. Deliverables must include comprehensive unit tests plus executable e2e script `tests/e2e/test_mission_tx_interfaces.sh` and detailed structured logs under `tests/e2e/logs/mission_tx_interfaces_*.jsonl` proving nominal, edge, and failure-path behavior.
8. Structured log contract for this bead requires fields: timestamp, component, scenario_id, correlation_id, decision_path, input_summary, outcome, reason_code, error_code,  {"id":"ft-1i2ge.8.9","title":"[mission][H9] Transaction observability, event taxonomy, and forensic bundle output","description":"## Objective
Define and implement tx observability so operators can diagnose failures without ambiguity.

## Scope
- Structured event taxonomy for tx plan/prepare/commit/compensate/show.
- Timeline reconstruction fields for forensics and replay linkage.
- Evidence bundle format for incident reports and CI artifacts.
- Redaction/privacy guarantees for sensitive command payload fragments.

## Deliverables
- Tx logging schema and event contracts.
- Bundle generator for deterministic artifact output.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Every tx transition emits structured logs with stable field contracts and correlation IDs.
2. Evidence bundle reconstructs full tx timeline and decision chain deterministically.
3. Redaction tests prove no secrets/PII leakage in logs and bundles.
4. Unit tests validate schema presence and required-field completeness.
5. Integration tests validate bundle generation from realistic tx runs.
6. Observability quality-gate checks fail when required tx signals are missing or malformed.
7. Deliverables include comprehensive unit tests plus executable e2e script `tests/e2e/test_mission_tx_observability.sh` and detailed structured logs under `tests/e2e/logs/mission_tx_observability_*.jsonl` proving nominal, edge, and failure-path behavior.
8. Output contracts are explicitly consumable by F7 telemetry integrity gates and by G-track go/no-go evidence generation.
9. Structured log contract for this bead requires fields: timestamp, component, scenario_id, correlation_id, decision_path, input_summary, outcome, reason_code, error_code, artifact_path.","notes":"Future-self note: if tx observability is weak, rollback incidents become expensive archaeology.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GrayIsland","created_at":"2026-02-22T23:29:33.819697Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-25T08:31:10.925466Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T08:31:10.925449Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e-tests","integration-tests","logging","mission","quality-gate","redaction","telemetry","test-coverage","test-logging","transactional-execution","tx","unit-tests","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.9","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.9","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.6.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.9","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.9","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1518,"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.9","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"tx_observability.rs delivered: TxEventKind taxonomy (15 variants), TxObservabilityEvent, TxTimelineEntry, TxForensicBundle with PlanSnapshot/LedgerSnapshot/ChainVerificationSummary/ResumeSummary.  {"id":"ft-1i50","title":"FTUI-09.4 Define go/no-go cutover review and acceptance pack","description":"## Background\nCutover should be a deliberate quality decision, not a gradual accidental switch.\n\n## Deliverables\n- go/no-go checklist (functional, perf, compatibility, resilience, docs)\n- required evidence artifacts and sign-off roles\n- final cutover readiness report template\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- cutover decision is evidence-based and auditable\n- unresolved blockers are explicit.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:10:57.918667842Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor","updated_at":"2026-02-09T05:38:54.194229929Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T05:38:54.194091222Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-1i50","depends_on_id":"ft-1f4u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1i50","depends_on_id":"ft-1q7m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1i50","depends_on_id":"ft-3ue0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1i50","depends_on_id":"ft-wzft","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-1i6d","title":"Process Tree Capture Per Pane","description":"## Goal
Capture the full process tree per pane (not just foreground process) for accurate session restoration, since AI agents spawn subprocesses that are critical to their operation.

## Background & Motivation
wa-32z7 (Process Re-launch) captures the foreground process per pane. But AI agents typically have deeper process trees:
```
pane 7:
  bash (shell)
    +-- claude (Claude Code CLI)
        +-- node (language server)
        +-- cargo check (compilation)
        +-- git status (version control)
```

If we only capture "claude" as the foreground process and restart it, the subprocesses are lost. The agent will restart but may be confused about the state of its subprocesses.

More importantly, the process tree reveals the agent's CURRENT ACTIVITY — if cargo is running, the agent is compiling; if git is running, it's checking version control. This information is valuable for snapshot decisions.

## Technical Design

### Process Tree Capture
```rust
pub struct ProcessTree {
    pub root: ProcessNode,
    pub total_processes: usize,
    pub total_threads: usize,
    pub total_rss_kb: u64,
}

pub struct ProcessNode {
    pub pid: u32,
    pub ppid: u32,
    pub name: String,
    pub cmdline: Vec<String>,
    pub state: ProcessState,  // Running, Sleeping, Zombie, etc.
    pub rss_kb: u64,
    pub cpu_time_ms: u64,
    pub children: Vec<ProcessNode>,
}
```

### Platform Implementation
Linux (/proc filesystem):
```rust
fn capture_process_tree(root_pid: u32) -> Result<ProcessTree> {
    // Read /proc/<pid>/stat for process info
    // Read /proc/<pid>/cmdline for command line
    // Read /proc/<pid>/status for RSS
    // Recursively walk /proc/<pid>/task/<tid>/children
    // Build tree from parent-child relationships
}
```

macOS (sysctl + libproc):
```rust
fn capture_process_tree_macos(root_pid: u32) -> Result<ProcessTree> {
    // Use proc_listchildpids() for children
    // Use proc_pidinfo() for process details
}
```

### Integration with Snapshot Schema
Extend PaneSnapshot (from bd-cuz) with process_tree field:
```rust
pub struct PaneSnapshot {
    // ... existing fields ...
    pub foreground_process: ProcessInfo,
    pub process_tree: Option<ProcessTree>,  // NEW
}
```

### Activity Inference from Process Tree
```rust
fn infer_activity(tree: &ProcessTree) -> PaneActivity {
    // If cargo/rustc running → Compiling
    // If git running → Version control
    // If npm/node running → JavaScript tooling
    // If python running → Script execution
    // If no children → Idle/waiting for input
}
```

This feeds into the priority classifier (wa-1qz1.5) as additional evidence.

### Implementation Location
- New: crates/wa-core/src/process_tree.rs
- Modify: snapshot schema (bd-cuz PaneSnapshot)
- Integration: ingest.rs captures tree periodically (every 30s, not every poll)

## Existing Code References
- bd-cuz: PaneSnapshot, ProcessInfo (extend with tree)
- wa-32z7: Process re-launch (uses tree for better restore)
- ingest.rs: capture pipeline (periodic tree capture)

## Configuration
```toml
[process_tree]
enabled = true
capture_interval_secs = 30    # How often to capture tree
max_depth = 5                 # Maximum tree depth
include_threads = false       # Include thread info (expensive)
```

## Dependencies
- bd-cuz (snapshot schema): extend PaneSnapshot
- Enhances wa-32z7 (process re-launch): more accurate restore
- Enhances wa-1qz1.5 (evidence ledger): activity inference as evidence

## Acceptance Criteria
- Full process tree capture per pane on Linux and macOS
- Process tree included in snapshots
- Activity inference from process tree
- Configurable capture frequency and depth
- Unit tests: mock /proc filesystem, verify tree construction
- Integration test: capture tree for real agent process

## macOS Platform Note
Process tree traversal must use platform-specific APIs:
- **Linux**: reads `/proc/<pid>/stat` for process info, `/proc/<pid>/cmdline` for command line, `/proc/<pid>/status` for RSS, and recursively walks `/proc/<pid>/task/<tid>/children` for child discovery.
- **macOS**: use `proc_pidinfo(pid, PROC_PIDLISTCHILDREN, ...)` or `proc_listchildpids(pid, ...)` from `libproc.h` to enumerate child processes. For process details, use `proc_pidinfo(pid, PROC_PIDTASKINFO, ...)` to get `proc_taskinfo` (RSS via `pti_resident_size`, CPU time via `pti_total_user + pti_total_system`). For command line, use `proc_pidinfo(pid, PROC_PIDPATHINFO, ...)` for the executable path and `sysctl([CTL_KERN, KERN_PROCARGS2, pid])` for full argv. As a simpler fallback, `pgrep -P <pid>` provides child PID enumeration without requiring FFI bindings.

## Benchmark Requirements
- **criterion benchmarks** with target `benches/process_tree.rs`:
  - `tree_traversal_latency`: measure time to capture the full process tree for a root PID with 5-20 children at depth 1-3. Target: <5ms for a typical agent process tree (shell + agent + 10 subprocesses).
  - `tree_serialization`: measure time to serialize ProcessTree to the snapshot format. Target: <100us for a 20-node tree.
  - `activity_inference`: measure time to classify activity from a process tree. Target: <10us per tree (simple pattern matching on process names).

## Property-Based Testing (proptest)
- **Tree consistency**: for any captured ProcessTree, every child node's `ppid` matches its parent's `pid`. The tree structure is always valid (no orphan nodes, no cycles).
- **Idempotent capture**: capturing the same process tree twice in quick succession (within 100ms) produces structurally identical trees (same PIDs, same parent-child relationships), assuming no process churn.
- **Depth limiting**: when `max_depth = D`, no node in the captured tree has depth > D. Deeper processes are silently omitted, not errored.
- **RSS aggregation**: `tree.total_rss_kb == sum of node.rss_kb for all nodes in tree`.
